headline: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) Faces Margin Pressure As Fortinet Gains Ground In SASE
summary: Palo Alto Networks is facing rising integration costs from recent large acquisitions, which are putting pressure on margins. These cost and margin issues are emerging as key near term risks that set the company apart from core cybersecurity peers. Rivals such as Fortinet are gaining traction with stronger adoption of unified SASE security platforms, sharpening competitive pressures. Palo Alto Networks, NasdaqGS:PANW, is drawing attention as higher integration costs from its recent...

headline: PANW vs. FTNT: Which Cybersecurity Stock Has an Edge Right Now?
summary: Fortinet gains an edge over Palo Alto Networks, supported by strong SASE adoption, steadier execution and a lower valuation.

headline: Will The Stock Split Spark CrowdStrike’s Next Big Run?
summary: Cybersecurity provider CrowdStrike (CRWD) authorized its first-ever stock split at a 4-for-1 ratio, scheduled for distribution after the market close on July 1. A single share, trading near $680, drops to approximately $170 after the split. The split follows a sustained operational recovery from the global IT outage of July 2024. CrowdStrike shares bottomed at $217.89 on August 2, 2024, under severe regulatory scrutiny. Over the next 22 months, the stock rebounded by more than 210%. The comeback
